algorithms.title: Post-ROSC Management Bundle
algorithms.description: Comprehensive post-ROSC management pathway from ROSC through neuroprognostication and recovery.
steps: ROSC achieved → confirm with waveform capnography (sudden rise in ETCO₂) and pulse check.
Airway management: confirm and secure advanced airway; target ETCO₂ 35–45 mmHg (avoid hypo/hyperventilation).
Hemodynamic support: IV fluid bolus (250–500 mL) + vasopressor (norepinephrine 0.1–0.5 mcg/kg/min) to MAP ≥65 mmHg.
Obtain 12-lead ECG immediately.
→ STEMI identified → emergent coronary angiography + PCI.
→ No STEMI → continue monitoring.
Non-contrast CT head to exclude intracranial hemorrhage.
Initiate temperature management: maintain ≤37.5°C at minimum.
→ If TTM chosen → 32–37.5°C for at least 36 hours.
→ Continuous temperature monitoring (esophageal, bladder, or PA probe).
→ Treat any fever >37.5°C immediately with antipyretics and active cooling.
→ Rewarming (if TTM used): ≤0.25°C per hour.
Identify and treat reversible causes (H's and T's).
Sedation/analgesia for comfort, temperature management, and ventilation.
Continuous monitoring: arterial line, ETCO₂, temperature, telemetry.
Neuroprognostication: DEFERRED to ≥72 hours after ROSC (or after rewarming).
→ Stop sedation and paralytics; allow metabolic clearance.
→ Multimodal assessment: clinical exam + SSEPs + EEG + NSE/S-100B + CT/MRI.
→ No single test is sufficient — use the combination.
Recovery planning: neurorehabilitation, cardiac rehabilitation, psychosocial support, family communication, advance care planning.

algorithms.title: ECPR Patient Selection and Initiation
algorithms.description: Decision pathway for extracorporeal CPR candidacy in refractory cardiac arrest.
steps: Refractory cardiac arrest: persistent VF/pVT despite standard ACLS (high-quality CPR, ≥3 shocks, epinephrine, amiodarone/lidocaine).
Identify potentially reversible cause: acute MI, PE, myocarditis, drug toxicity, hypothermia, tension pneumothorax.
If no reversible cause identified → continue standard ACLS; consider cessation criteria per institutional protocol.
Reversible cause present → evaluate ECPR candidacy.
Is expected low-flow time <60 minutes? (Time from start of CPR to ECMO flow)
NO → ECPR unlikely to benefit; continue standard ACLS.
YES → Is the patient a suitable candidate? (age, comorbidities, functional status, no absolute contraindications)
NO → continue standard ACLS.
YES → Activate ECPR team.
Rapid femoral cannulation: venous drainage (femoral vein) + arterial return (femoral artery) under ultrasound guidance.
Place distal perfusion catheter to prevent limb ischemia.
Initiate VA-ECMO flow and confirm adequate perfusion.
Continue resuscitation on ECMO support.
Obtain 12-lead ECG → if STEMI → emergent PCI.
Non-contrast CT head → exclude hemorrhage.
Initiate post-ROSC management bundle (airway, hemodynamics, temperature).
Weaning: guided by echocardiographic recovery of native cardiac function, hemodynamic stability on decreasing ECMO flow, and clinical assessment.
